category: object-store / model-registry (POSIX-over-object FS, S3 gateway for AI training data)
backup_api_paths: / (GET ListAllMyBuckets - S3 service root, returns ListAllMyBucketsResult XML)
/metrics (port 9567, juicefs_* Prometheus metrics - reveals volume name + activity)
ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML payload from NAMES/MANIFEST only - never download objects. (1) ListAllMyBucketsResult XML from GET / enumerates bucket names; AI/ML signal = buckets named models, datasets, checkpoints, mlflow, artifacts, training-data, weights, embeddings, runs, lakefs, dvc-cache, huggingface. (2) ListObjectsV2 <Key> prefixes (read keys only, do not GET objects): model.safetensors, pytorch_model.bin, *.ckpt / *.pt / *.pth / *.onnx / *.gguf, config.json + tokenizer.json pairs (HF layout), mlflow tracking dirs (artifacts/<run-id>/), DVC .dir manifests, lakeFS _lakefs/ refs. (3) The /metrics juicefs_* labels carry vol_name and mp (mount point) - a volume named e.g. ml-training or model-store is itself the AI-scope tell. Because JuiceFS is a POSIX FS surfaced as S3, object keys mirror real training-data directory trees, so the key namespace alone classifies the store without any object read.
fingerprint_markers: Prometheus /metrics on :9567 with the vendor-unique juicefs_ metric prefix (juicefs_uptime, juicefs_used_space, juicefs_blockcache_blocks, juicefs_object_request_durations_histogram_seconds, juicefs_cpu_usage) - MinIO uses minio_/go_ prefixes, never juicefs_
juicefs_* metric labels expose vol_name / mp (mount point) - the JuiceFS volume identity, absent from any native MinIO deployment

S3 service on :9000 derived from juicedata/minio fork (gateway-1.0 branch): MinIO-style Server header + x-amz-request-id + x-minio-* response headers, but co-located with a :9567 juicefs_ metrics port on the same host
Web console (MinIO Console fork) reachable via --console-address (commonly :59001) - login page is MinIO Console, NOT JuiceFS-unique on its own
Anonymous-listable buckets respond to ListObjectsV2 with HTTP 200 + ListBucketResult XML and no AWS SigV4 header (anon policy set to download/public via mc anonymous)
fp_guards: DO NOT fingerprint on port 9000 alone or on the MinIO Server header alone - the S3 surface is a MinIO fork and is byte-indistinguishable from native MinIO at the S3 layer. The discriminator MUST be the co-resident :9567 /metrics endpoint with the juicefs_ prefix.
minio_ or minio_node_ or minio_cluster_ metric prefix => native MinIO, NOT JuiceFS - exclude.
MinIO Console login page or /minio/admin/v3 paths WITHOUT a juicefs_ /metrics neighbor => plain MinIO; the console is the same fork and is not a positive JuiceFS marker by itself.
A juicefs_ /metrics endpoint with NO :9000 S3 service => juicefs mount (FUSE) or CSI driver, NOT the S3 gateway - reclassify, do not count as gateway exposure.

9000 also collides with native MinIO, Portainer, SonarQube, and Prometheus-pushgateway-adjacent services; require BOTH the S3 ListBucketResult XML on 9000 AND the juicefs_ metrics on 9567 before labeling JuiceFS gateway.

A 200 with ListBucketResult that lists buckets but every bucket ListObjects returns AccessDenied => surface open, anonymous LISTING not exercised; record as 'service-root anon, bucket access not exercised', not as anon-readable data.

cves.desc: Transitive Go stdlib database/sql Rows.Scan race condition (CWE-362, CVSS 7.0). Surfaces in JuiceFS only as a Go-toolchain advisory (fixed in distro pkg 1.2.4-r3); not a gateway-specific or remotely-triggerable JuiceFS vuln. Build-version signal, not an exploit path.

Command injection in the Fluid project's JuicefsRuntime (authenticated user executes arbitrary OS commands in juicefs containers). Affects Fluid's CSI integration, NOT the standalone juicefs gateway binary - scope-adjacent, do not attribute to the S3 gateway surface.

INHERITED-FROM-MINIO CANDIDATE: JuiceFS gateway is a juicedata/minio (gateway-1.0) fork; pre-RELEASE.2023-03-20 MinIO leaks all env vars (incl. MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD / secret-key) via POST /minio/bootstrap/v1/verify. Applies only if the fork predates the fix - VERIFY the embedded MinIO RELEASE date per host before asserting; mark CANDIDATE until the bootstrap path returns env data.

pivot_paths: GET / on :9000 -> ListAllMyBucketsResult: enumerate bucket names only (AI-scope tell), no object reads
GET /<bucket>?policy -> read anonymous bucket policy to know download/public exposure WITHOUT listing contents
GET /<bucket>/?list-type=2 -> read object KEY prefixes only on anon-readable buckets (model/dataset/checkpoint layout classification)

GET :9567/metrics -> read juicefs_* gauges: vol_name label (volume identity), juicefs_used_space (store scale), juicefs_uptime (operator longevity), object-request histograms (live-vs-stale activity) - all read-only telemetry
Console URL (--console-address, often :59001) -> confirm MinIO Console fork presence read-only; do NOT submit credentials
Cross-reference vol_name from /metrics against bucket names from ListBuckets to attribute operator/project - names-only correlation
restraint_note: Names and manifests ARE the finding. Enumerate bucket names, object KEY prefixes, anon policies, and juicefs_ metric labels (vol_name, mp) only. Do NOT GET/download any object body - object content is training data / model weights and pulling it is exfiltration. Do NOT write objects, create buckets, set or change bucket/anon policy, or submit console credentials. A bucket that 200s to ListObjectsV2 without a SigV4 header is anon-listable and the name list is sufficient proof; if listing returns AccessDenied, record 'surface open, anonymous access not exercised' - never brute or sign requests to force a read. Sample an object only to the minimum needed to confirm severity, and only with explicit per-engagement authorization.
